info.title: Didit Credentials API
info.description: OpenID4VCI 1.0 issuance + OpenID4VP 1.0 verification (SD-JWT VC, draft-16) with a multi-tenant management plane: credentials, presentations, verifications, a trust registry (frameworks, trusted issuers, relying parties), and issuer key rotation. Management endpoints authenticate with a tenant API key (`Authorization: Bearer <key>`); the OpenID4VCI/VP protocol + holder/verifier endpoints are public.

get.description: Fetch a Token Status List (whole list)
get.summary: Fetch a Token Status List (whole list)

impact.note: Existing credentials remain valid under the rotated key until valid_until.

post.description: Issue a c_nonce for the credential proof
post.summary: Issue a c_nonce for the credential proof

SAMLCheckSlugRequest.description: Serializer for checking if an organization slug has SAML configured.

slug.description: The organization slug to check for SAML configuration.

has_saml.description: Whether the organization has SAML configured

idp_entity_id.description: The Entity ID of the Identity Provider (from IdP metadata).

idp_sso_url.description: The Single Sign-On URL of the Identity Provider.

idp_slo_url.description: The Single Logout URL of the Identity Provider (optional).

is_enabled.description: Whether SAML SSO is enabled for this organization.

enforce_sso.description: Require all organization members to use SAML SSO (disable password login).

allowed_email_domains.description: List of VERIFIED email domains allowed to authenticate via this SAML configuration. Domains must be verified via DNS TXT record before they can be added here. Example: ['company.com', 'subsidiary.company.com'].

SAMLConfigUpdateRequest.description:
Serializer for creating/updating SAML configuration.

NOTE: allowed_email_domains cannot be set directly here.
Domains must be verified via DNS TXT record before they can be used.
Use the domain verification endpoints to add domains.

provider.description: The identity provider type (okta, onelogin, azure_ad, etc.)

idp_entity_id.description: The Entity ID of your Identity Provider (from IdP metadata)

idp_sso_url.description: The Single Sign-On URL of your Identity Provider

idp_slo_url.description: The Single Logout URL of your Identity Provider (optional)

idp_x509_cert.description: The X.509 certificate of your Identity Provider (PEM format). Required for new configurations.

is_enabled.description: Whether to enable SAML SSO. NOTE: Requires at least one verified domain. Verify domain ownership first using the domain verification endpoints.

enforce_sso.description: Enforce SAML authentication for the entire organization (disable password login for all members)

attribute_mapping.description: Custom mapping of SAML attributes to user fields

redirect_uri.description: The URL to redirect to after SAML authentication.

device_fingerprint.description: Unique fingerprint to identify the device (for multi-device support).

redirect_url.description: URL to redirect user to for SAML authentication

metadata_url.description: URL to fetch IdP metadata from.

SAMLSPInfo.description: Service Provider information for IdP configuration.

entity_id.description: The Entity ID (Audience URI) to configure in your IdP

acs_url.description: The Assertion Consumer Service URL - where IdP sends SAML Response

metadata_url.description: URL to download SP metadata XML

SAMLTokenExchangeRequest.description: Serializer for exchanging SAML authorization code for tokens.

code.description: The one-time authorization code received after SAML authentication.

Serializer for staff organization search results.
Returns detailed organization information for staff users.
